design_rationale: The reward uses a centered position term for lateral guidance, a conditional descent-speed penalty for soft landing preparation, an upright stability term for attitude control, and a fuel-cost penalty for efficiency. Sparse events provide a large success bonus for simultaneous two-leg soft upright touchdown and a large crash penalty for extreme conditions or leaving the viewport. The combination is intended to shape the full approach sequence.
assumptions: Coordinates are normalized: x in approximately [-1,1], landing pad at (0,0) with flags nearby.
The lander starts with random initial thrust; dense rewards apply from the first step.
Left and right leg contact booleans reliably indicate ground contact.
The environment terminates episode on crash or exiting viewport, making sparse event triggers feasible.
Conditional component y<0.5 selects the lower half of the viewport where descent guidance matters.
risk_notes: The agent may exploit centering term by hovering; fuel penalty must be large enough to counteract.
No penalty for high horizontal velocity until touchdown; a fast skid could still satisfy success if vx low at contact.
Crash threshold (vy>0.8, angle>0.8) may be high, missing moderate crashes; later tuning will tighten limits.
Centering reward only covers x, not y proximity to pad; agent could be high and still collect reward, potentially delaying descent.
All dense components are linear/absolute-value based, which may cause high-speed jittering if weights are too high.
